QTWO.value_chain_position: Banking software platform layer — sells cloud-based digital banking and lending infrastructure (online/mobile banking, lending origination, account opening) to community banks and credit unions.
QTWO.relative_comps: Q2 is the leading independent digital banking platform for community/regional FIs, competing against legacy core giants FIS and Fiserv and the focused nCino in lending. Its 14% YoY revenue growth and 24% FCF margin are solid but below nCino's growth rate; it commands a meaningful premium over legacy players given cloud-native architecture and switching costs. The ~$2.8B market cap represents modest premium to recurring revenue, leaving room vs. FIS/Fiserv on relative growth but trading tighter than pure-SaaS peers.

CALX.value_chain_position: Broadband platform software and systems layer — sells cloud-and-software platform (Calix Cloud) plus access hardware to community broadband providers (telcos, rural ISPs, co-ops), enabling subscriber intelligence and revenue operations.
CALX.relative_comps: Calix is repositioning from a hardware vendor to a platform-software company serving community broadband operators; 28% YoY growth reflects that mix shift and the rural broadband BEAD tailwind. It competes with Adtran on the hardware side and lacks a direct platform comp, giving it pricing power in a fragmented addressable market. FCF margin at 10% is thin vs. pure-software peers, and the 3Y CAGR of 5% reflects the transitional period; the high YoY acceleration is the key signal.

GRND.value_chain_position: Social networking / dating application — operates a location-based LGBTQ+ social and dating platform monetized via subscriptions and advertising.
GRND.relative_comps: Grindr is profitable and fast-growing (31% YoY, 30% FCF margin) but operates in the consumer social/dating vertical with no fit to any disruption theme in the taxonomy. Its competitive moat is community network effects in a specific demographic niche; it competes against Match Group and Bumble on dating and Meta for social attention. Strong unit economics but the FMP industry filter misfiled it alongside genuine software infrastructure plays.

APP.value_chain_position: AI-native advertising and monetization platform layer — sells an AI-driven programmatic ad engine (AXON) that acts as a performance marketing toll-taker between mobile/connected-TV app publishers and advertisers.
APP.relative_comps: AppLovin is the clearest chokepoint in mobile app performance marketing: AXON's AI targeting creates compounding data moats and 51% YoY revenue growth with 71% FCF margin is best-in-class among scaled ad-tech platforms. It trades at a steep premium to TTD and Criteo on EV/FCF, reflecting the growth divergence; Google and Meta are the ceiling competitors but are constrained by antitrust posture, creating structural runway. The ai_infrastructure tag is warranted because AXON is a deployed-inference product paid per auction.

SHOP.value_chain_position: Commerce operating system and payments rail layer — provides the end-to-end merchant infrastructure stack (storefront, payments, lending, logistics) and collects a toll on every transaction processed through Shopify Payments and Shopify Capital.
SHOP.relative_comps: Shopify is the dominant SMB-to-mid-market commerce OS with 32% YoY revenue growth and deepening payments penetration (Merchant Solutions now ~75% of revenue) creating a self-reinforcing take-rate toll. It competes against BigCommerce and Wix on the low end and Salesforce/SAP on enterprise; Amazon is both a competitor and channel. FCF margin of 17% is expanding as payments mix grows. The multiple reflects compounder status but Shopify's network-effect moat in payments data is underappreciated in comps.

ADBE.value_chain_position: Creative and document intelligence platform layer — sells the dominant creative toolchain (Photoshop, Illustrator, Premiere, Firefly generative AI) and document workflow OS (Acrobat/PDF) that underlies all professional content creation and AI-augmented design.
ADBE.relative_comps: Adobe is the incumbent creative monopoly with exceptional FCF conversion (42% FCF margin) but growth has slowed to ~11% YoY as generative AI competitors (Canva, Figma, Midjourney) erode the consumer/SMB edge. Enterprise and PDF/document intelligence remain sticky, and Firefly is a genuine AI moat in licensed training data. At a $96B cap it trades at a discount to pure-cloud SaaS on revenue multiple, reflecting the maturing growth trajectory; the ai_infrastructure tag reflects Firefly/AXON-equivalent inference monetization embedded in Creative Cloud.

INTU.value_chain_position: Financial management platform layer — operates the dominant SMB accounting/payroll OS (QuickBooks), consumer tax filing (TurboTax), and Credit Karma financial data network, collectively the operating layer for small business finance and consumer financial decisions.
INTU.relative_comps: Intuit is the clearest toll-taker in small-business financial software: QuickBooks commands 85%+ market share in US SMB accounting and TurboTax dominates assisted tax. The 15% YoY growth reflects Credit Karma monetization and QuickBooks Online international expansion; FCF margin of 37% is best-in-class. At $80B it trades at a significant premium to Xero and Bill.com on EV/FCF but the moat durability and platform ecosystem lock-in justify the posture.

ADSK.value_chain_position: Engineering design and simulation platform layer — sells the dominant CAD/CAM/BIM toolchain (AutoCAD, Revit, Fusion 360) used to design physical products, buildings, and factories, increasingly integrating digital twin and generative design for manufacturing and construction.
ADSK.relative_comps: Autodesk is the workflow monopoly in AEC (architecture/engineering/construction) and a dominant position in manufacturing CAD; 18% YoY growth reflects subscription model maturation and Fusion 360 penetration in manufacturing. Competing against Siemens/Dassault Systemes in enterprise PLM and PTC in industrial IoT adjacency. FCF margin of 36% is superior to PTC and BSY; the robotics_automation tag is grounded in Fusion 360's generative design for manufacturing and direct integration with robot simulation workflows.

WDAY.value_chain_position: Enterprise HR and financial management platform layer — sells the cloud-native ERP system of record (HCM, Financial Management, Planning) to large enterprises, embedding AI-driven workforce analytics and financial close automation.
WDAY.relative_comps: Workday is the premier independent cloud HR/Finance platform with 13% YoY growth and 30% FCF margin, competing directly against SAP SuccessFactors and Oracle HCM in the enterprise segment. Its 3Y CAGR of 15% and sticky renewal economics (NRR >100%) support a premium multiple. Leverage (net funded debt/EBITDA ~1.9x) is slightly elevated but manageable; ServiceNow is an expansion threat in workflows. The fintech_rails tag reflects the Financial Management Cloud acting as a toll-taker on enterprise treasury and close workflows.

TEAM.value_chain_position: Developer workflow and collaboration platform layer — sells the OS for software development teams (Jira, Confluence, Bitbucket, Rovo AI) used as the system of record for engineering work across virtually every cloud-native organization.
TEAM.relative_comps: Atlassian is deeply embedded in developer workflows with 25% YoY growth and 19% FCF margin; it competes most directly with Microsoft/GitHub in DevOps and Notion/Linear in the next-gen collaboration market. The Rovo AI agent layer (launched 2025) is the critical bet on becoming the AI-orchestration layer for enterprise dev teams, warranting the ai_infrastructure theme. Net cash position provides M&A flexibility; the $25B cap is compressed relative to growth vs. ServiceNow at a much larger scale.

PTC.value_chain_position: Industrial software and IoT platform layer — sells PLM, CAD (Creo), and industrial IoT (ThingWorx) software that orchestrates the full product lifecycle and real-time factory floor data, directly enabling connected factory and robotics deployments.
PTC.relative_comps: PTC is the clearest software toll-taker in industrial automation: ThingWorx/Vuforia power the connected factory layer that every robot deployment needs for monitoring and digital twin management. Its 28% YoY growth reflects the Servicemax/Arena acquisitions and IoT platform ramp; 31% FCF margin is strong for an industrial software mix. It competes with Siemens Digital Industries and Dassault in PLM but has a differentiated edge in AR-assisted maintenance. The robotics_automation theme fit is genuine, not FMP filter noise.

BSY.value_chain_position: Infrastructure engineering software platform layer — sells the dominant CAD/simulation tools (MicroStation, OpenRoads, OpenBuildings) for civil infrastructure (roads, bridges, utilities, rail) and increasingly digital twin platforms (iTwin) for asset lifecycle management.
BSY.relative_comps: Bentley occupies a focused niche in civil/infrastructure engineering software where ADSK has limited penetration; iTwin digital twin platform is the key growth driver but still early. 12% YoY growth and 32% FCF margin are solid for its scale; leverage at 2.2x EBITDA is notable. The robotics_automation tag is marginal — iTwin more closely supports energy_transition infrastructure builds than autonomous robotics, but the digital twin layer for infrastructure is genuinely disruptive in the construction/ops workflow.

IDCC.value_chain_position: Wireless standards IP licensing layer — holds essential patents on 3G/4G/5G/Wi-Fi/video codec standards and licenses them to device OEMs (Apple, Samsung, Xiaomi) on a per-unit royalty basis.
IDCC.relative_comps: InterDigital is a pure IP licensing business with exceptional FCF margin (70%) but only 1.7% YoY revenue growth as the 5G licensing cycle matures. It is not a disruption platform participant — it is a royalty toll on device shipments already priced into OEM BOMs. While it benefits from 5G and Wi-Fi 7 cycles, it does not occupy any value-chain layer in the taxonomy's themes; the FMP filter caught it as Software - Application but it is fundamentally a patent portfolio business.

DSGX.value_chain_position: Supply chain logistics network platform layer — operates a cloud-based multi-carrier transportation management and customs compliance network connecting shippers, carriers, and customs agencies on a transaction-toll model.
DSGX.relative_comps: Descartes is a highly sticky B2B logistics network operator with 13% YoY growth and 37% FCF margin, compounding through M&A of specialized compliance and routing networks. Its competitive moat is the multi-directional network effect (each new carrier/customs node makes the platform more valuable to all participants). It competes with E2open on the TMS side and project44 on visibility; the fintech_rails tag is apt since every shipment triggers a compliance and data toll. Capital-light model with negative net debt.

APPF.value_chain_position: Property management SaaS platform layer — sells the operating system for residential property managers (leasing, maintenance, accounting, payments) and collects payments/fintech revenue on top of software fees.
true_competitors: REAL Page (private, acquired by Thoma Bravo)
APPF.relative_comps: AppFolio serves the underserved SMB/mid-market property manager segment (1-5k units) that Yardi and RealPage deprioritize; 21% YoY growth with 23% FCF margin reflects AI-driven leasing automation and ARPU expansion. The competitive set is mostly private (Yardi, Entrata, RealPage) so APPF is the only pure-play public comps. The fintech_rails tag reflects payments and insurance distribution embedded in the workflow as toll-taker revenue. Relatively low load-bearing score as switching is painful but technically feasible at mid-market scale.

PCTY.value_chain_position: Mid-market HCM and payroll platform layer — sells cloud-native HR, payroll, and benefits administration to US mid-market employers (20-1000 employees), earning float income plus software and payment-processing fees.
PCTY.relative_comps: Paylocity competes directly with Paychex and ADP in the mid-market HCM segment with a more modern UX and superior NPS; 15% YoY growth and 27% FCF margin reflect stable compounding with payroll float income contributing defensively. Growth is moderating from its post-COVID 23% CAGR peak. The fintech_rails tag is justified by payroll float and benefits payment-rail economics; load-bearing score is 3 because ADP/Paychex and Gusto offer credible alternatives at the margin.

DUOL.value_chain_position: Consumer language learning application layer — operates a freemium gamified language learning app monetized via subscriptions (Duolingo Super/Max) and advertising, with an AI-native Max tier powered by GPT-4/Claude models.
DUOL.relative_comps: Duolingo is the clear market leader in consumer language learning with exceptional metrics (35% YoY growth, 38% FCF margin, $1.1B revenue) but does not fit any disruption theme in the taxonomy — it is a consumer app business, not a platform/infrastructure toll-taker in AI, energy, robotics, genomics, defense, fintech, or space. The AI feature (Max) enhances the product but does not make Duolingo an AI infrastructure participant. Growth and quality metrics are excellent; theme fit is genuinely absent.

CVLT.value_chain_position: Data protection and cyber resilience platform layer — sells enterprise backup, recovery, and data security software (Commvault Cloud, Metallic SaaS) that protects the data layer of every AI and cloud deployment from ransomware and data loss.
CVLT.relative_comps: Commvault is the incumbent enterprise data protection vendor transitioning to a cloud/SaaS model (Metallic), competing against fast-growing pure-cloud challengers Rubrik and Cohesity. 19% YoY growth and 20% FCF margin are improving but lag Rubrik's growth trajectory; its multicloud/hybrid coverage and air-gap recovery capabilities are the key differentiators in the AI era where data integrity is mission-critical. The ai_infrastructure tag reflects data protection as a required infrastructure layer for every AI deployment.

SRAD.value_chain_position: Sports data intelligence platform layer — aggregates, cleans, and distributes real-time sports data and betting integrity services to sports betting operators, media rights holders, and leagues on a licensing/SaaS model.
SRAD.relative_comps: Sportradar is the global toll-taker in official sports data rights, monetizing the proliferation of regulated sports betting globally; 15% YoY growth and 18% FCF margin are solid for a data licensing business. It competes with Genius Sports (the other public comps) and private Stats Perform/IMG Arena. The business does not fit any of the seven disruption themes in the taxonomy — it is a data rights/licensing business in sports/gambling, not a technology infrastructure enabler in the defined themes.

MNDY.value_chain_position: Work OS and AI automation platform layer — sells a no-code/low-code workflow automation platform (monday.com Work OS) used to orchestrate cross-functional business processes, increasingly with AI agents (monday AI) embedded in workflow nodes.
MNDY.relative_comps: monday.com is the fastest-growing scaled work management platform (25% YoY, 24% FCF margin, net cash of $1.5B+) competing against Asana and Smartsheet on the SMB/mid-market and ServiceNow on the enterprise workflow side. The AI agents layer (monday AI) positions it as an agentic workflow orchestration tool, giving it a credible ai_infrastructure claim as enterprises use it to deploy business-process AI agents. The $4.3B cap looks modest relative to its growth and FCF profile vs. comparables.
